data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Holdings Reach $3.792 Billion
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass reveals that the Hyperliquid platform's whale currently holds positions valued at $3.792 billion. The long positions amount to $1.882 billion, representing 49.64% of the total, while short positions are at $1.91 billion, accounting for 50.36%. The long positions have incurred a loss of $22.7988 million, whereas the short positions have gained $53.0511 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has taken a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2,148.7, with an unrealized profit and loss of $496,600.

data.title: Meta Reintroduces Stock Options for Executives Amid AI Competition
data.description: Meta is reintroducing stock options for its top executives for the first time since its initial public offering in 2012. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of the company's strategy to retain and reward its leadership team. This decision comes as Meta continues to invest heavily in artificial intelligence to maintain its competitive edge in the rapidly evolving tech landscape. The company aims to ensure that its executives are aligned with its long-term goals as it navigates the challenges and opportunities presented by the AI sector.

data.title: Thailand's Finance Minister: Government to Allow Energy Prices to Fluctuate with Market Mechanisms
data.description: Thailand's Finance Minister has announced that the government will permit energy prices to fluctuate freely according to market mechanisms. According to Jin10, this decision reflects a shift towards a more market-driven approach in managing energy costs. The move is expected to impact various sectors, potentially leading to adjustments in consumer prices and business operations. The Finance Minister emphasized the importance of aligning energy pricing with global market trends to ensure economic stability and competitiveness. This policy change comes amid broader efforts to enhance Thailand's economic resilience and adaptability in the face of fluctuating global energy markets.

data.title: Thailand's Finance Minister Plans Measures to Mitigate Rising Fuel Prices
data.description: Thailand's Finance Minister has announced plans to introduce support measures aimed at alleviating the impact of rising fuel prices. According to Jin10, the government is considering various strategies to address the economic challenges posed by increasing fuel costs. These measures are expected to provide relief to consumers and businesses affected by the price hikes. The move comes as part of a broader effort to stabilize the economy and ensure sustainable growth amid global economic uncertainties. Further details on the specific measures and their implementation timeline are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Oil Prices Drop Over 5% as Gold Rises Amid U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Talks
data.description:
International oil prices have fallen by more than 5% to over 6%, while gold prices have increased by over 2%. According to RTHK, reports indicate that the United States is seeking a one-month ceasefire with Iran. This development has fueled market hopes for the resumption of oil exports from the Persian Gulf.

During Asian trading hours, both Brent crude and U.S. crude futures experienced declines. Brent crude was recently priced at $97.87 per barrel, marking a 6.3% decrease. Meanwhile, New York crude was trading at $87.3 per barrel, reflecting a drop of approximately 5.5%.

In contrast, spot gold prices have surged, nearing $4,600 per ounce. Earlier, gold was priced at $4,585.1 per ounce, representing a 2.5% increase.

data.title: Analysts predict oil prices will remain high until the conflict with Iran ends.
data.description: Oil prices fell sharply as the US pushed for an end to the war with Iran. Ajay Parmar, head of oil markets at commodities research firm ICIS, said, "Oil prices will continue to fluctuate because the market is influenced by both fundamental factors and rhetoric. The fundamentals haven't changed—there are still nearly 10 million barrels per day of oil supply disruptions in the Middle East, and we expect oil prices to remain high until this war ends." (Jinshi)

data.title: Disney CEO Faces Challenges with Billion-Dollar Tech Investments
data.description: Disney CEO Josh D’Amaro, who recently assumed his position, is already encountering significant challenges as two major technology investments face setbacks. Bloomberg posted on X that one of these investments has completely unraveled, marking a difficult start for D’Amaro in his new role. The situation underscores the complexities and risks associated with large-scale technological ventures in the entertainment industry. As Disney navigates these challenges, the company's strategic direction under D’Amaro's leadership will be closely watched by industry analysts and investors.

data.title: Bitcoin Wallet Linked to Clifton Collins Activated After Decade of Inactivity
data.description: A Bitcoin wallet associated with Clifton Collins has reportedly been activated after approximately 10 years of dormancy, with 500 BTC transferred to a different address. According to NS3.AI, Collins had amassed 6,000 BTC across 12 addresses. The recent transaction indicates that not all of these holdings were lost.

data.title: House Republicans Reject Senate's Legislative Proposal
data.description: House Republicans have declined a legislative proposal from the Senate. According to Odaily, the proposal suggested incorporating the House's bipartisan community bank deregulation bill into pending cryptocurrency market structure legislation in exchange for the House's acceptance of the Senate-passed affordable housing bill. Rep. Bill Huizenga, Vice Chair of the House Financial Services Committee, expressed skepticism about the proposal. Last week, Senate Banking Committee Republicans discussed the proposal in a closed-door meeting, with Senator Katie Britt assisting in presenting it to other Republican senators. The House had included community bank deregulation provisions in a housing bill passed in February, but the Senate's housing bill passed this month did not include these provisions. House Republicans stated that several issues remain in the Senate's housing bill, and simply adding the banking bill to cryptocurrency legislation is insufficient for them to accept the Senate's version of the housing bill. Rep. Andy Barr, a senior member of the House Financial Services Committee, supports including banking regulation provisions in cryptocurrency legislation but also demands that the Senate incorporate parts of the House's housing proposals.

data.title: Bitcoin Linked to Irish Drug Dealer Moves After Nearly a Decade
data.description: According to Odaily, 500 BTC associated with Irish drug dealer Clifton Collins have been moved on the blockchain after being considered lost for nearly 10 years. Collins acquired 6,000 BTC between 2011 and 2012 using proceeds from cannabis sales, distributing them across 12 wallets. He recorded the private keys on paper, which he hid in a fishing rod case at a rental property in Galway, Ireland. Following his arrest in 2017, the property was cleared, and his belongings were sent to a landfill. Both authorities and Collins believed the keys were permanently lost, leading the court to confiscate the funds as criminal proceeds. The recent transfer of 500 BTC suggests that at least part of the assets may not have been lost.

data.title: USD.AI Expands Support for Ethereum-Based Stablecoin Transactions
data.description: USD.AI, a stablecoin protocol offering credit for AI, has announced new support for users to mint USDai and sUSDai on the Ethereum network using USDC. According to Foresight News, the exchange between USDC and PYUSD is facilitated by LI.FI and routed through a pool managed by Spark on Curve Finance to ensure minimal slippage.

data.title: Heineken to Reduce Beer Production in Singapore by 2027
data.description: Heineken plans to scale down its large-scale beer production in Singapore by 2027. Bloomberg posted on X, the company intends to transfer its production operations to breweries located in Malaysia and Vietnam. This strategic move is part of Heineken's broader efforts to optimize its production capabilities in the region. The decision reflects the company's focus on enhancing efficiency and leveraging the capabilities of its facilities in neighboring countries. By shifting production, Heineken aims to better align its resources with market demands and operational goals.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that "SpaceX will IPO before June 30" has risen to 75%, a 32% increase in the past 24 hours.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that the probability of SpaceX IPO before June 30th has risen to 75%, a 32% increase in 24 hours. Furthermore, the probability of SpaceX IPO before September 30th is currently reported at 86%, and the probability of SpaceX IPO before December 31st is currently reported at 90%. According to The Information, Elon Musk's SpaceX plans to file for an IPO as early as this week. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Trump said Vance is involved in negotiations with Iran to end the war.
data.description: US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are leading negotiations with Iran and expressed optimism about reaching an agreement. Iranian sources told CNN that Tehran is willing to listen to "sustainable" proposals to end the war. Meanwhile, Israel launched a new round of strikes against Iran, and Tehran also launched an attack on Tel Aviv. An Israeli official said that an agreement "does not seem realistic at the moment." Two regional sources said that Iran has told the Trump administration that it does not want to restart negotiations with Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, but prefers to contact Vice President JD Vance. In addition, approximately 1,000 US Army 82nd Airborne Division soldiers are expected to be deployed to the Middle East in the coming days. Republicans again vetoed a resolution requiring Trump to seek congressional authorization for future military action against Iran.

data.title: Democrat Wins Florida Special Election, Signaling Challenges for Republicans
data.description: A Democrat is set to join the Florida House of Representatives following an unexpected Republican loss in a special election. According to Jin10, Decision Desk HQ predicts that Democratic candidate Emily Gregory will defeat her Republican opponent, Jon Maples, in the election held on Tuesday. While Republicans maintain a strong majority in the Florida legislature, this unexpected outcome serves as a symbolic victory for Democrats and a warning for Republicans. As the November congressional midterm elections approach, Republicans face a challenging battle to retain their majority in both the U.S. House of Representatives and the Senate.

data.title: Supply Chain Attack on Litellm Poses Significant Security Risks
data.description: Andrej Karpathy recently reported on the X platform that litellm has been subjected to a PyPI supply chain attack. According to Odaily, executing a simple 'pip install litellm' can lead to the theft of SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure credentials, Kubernetes configurations, git credentials, environment variables, encrypted wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD keys, and database passwords. Litellm has a monthly download rate of 97 million, and the risk extends to all projects dependent on litellm, such as dspy. The malicious code was online for less than an hour before being discovered due to a flaw that caused Callum McMahon's machine to crash from memory exhaustion. Karpathy highlighted that supply chain attacks are among the most threatening issues in modern software, as installing dependencies can introduce compromised packages deep within the dependency tree. He is increasingly inclined to reduce dependencies and use LLMs to directly implement simple functions.

data.title: WTI Crude Oil Falls Below $88 Per Barrel
data.description: WTI crude oil prices have dropped below $88 per barrel, experiencing a 1.01% decline. According to Odaily, this marks a significant decrease in the daily trading session.

data.title: STOCKS | Taiwan's Taiex Index Surges Amid Asian Market Recovery
data.description: Taiwan's Taiex index experienced a significant rise, climbing as much as 3.3% as Asian markets broadly rebounded. Bloomberg posted on X that the surge is attributed to growing expectations of a de-escalation in the Iran conflict, which has positively influenced investor sentiment across the region.

data.title: House Republicans Reject Senate-Backed Crypto Legislation Deal
data.description: House Republicans have turned down a Senate-supported agreement that aimed to link cryptocurrency market structure legislation with a housing package. According to NS3.AI, the proposal also sought to incorporate the House's community bank deregulation bill into the crypto measure. House Republicans expressed concerns over unresolved issues in the Senate's housing bill and are advocating for the inclusion of certain House housing proposals.

data.title: Jump Trading Seeks Dismissal of $4 Billion Lawsuit from Terraform Labs Trust
data.description: Jump Trading has requested a court to dismiss a $4 billion lawsuit filed by the Terraform Labs bankruptcy trust. According to NS3.AI, the lawsuit accuses Jump Trading of market manipulation, fraud, and self-dealing. Jump Trading argues that the case is an effort to transfer liability related to Terraform Labs' $4.4 billion penalty imposed by the SEC.
